rationale: The cited paper is used as a concrete methodological source, not just background. In the model section, the authors explicitly state they "follow Naik and Tsai (2000)" to handle endogeneity in ad impressions, and they implement a related measurement-error/correlated-error structure in Equations 6-7. The empirical results then evaluate those endogeneity correlations, showing this borrowed framework is part of the paper's estimation logic. This gives the citation a clear functional role in model specification and inference.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Naik and Tsai's measurement-error/endogeneity-control approach within its dynamic digital advertising model. It adapts that framework to impression-click panel data to model correlated unobservables and justify endogeneity correction.
